title: cassine anjouanensis
intro: Cassine anjouanensis, also known as the Anjouanese cassine, is a plant species native to the island of Anjouan in the Comoros archipelago. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cassine anjouanensis include its potential to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant can be used in herbal preparations like tea, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in some traditional remedies in the Comoros islands.
